notes: SKGLView renders correctly on both desktop Chromium and iOS Safari Simulator with SkiaSharp 3.119.2. All drawing operations (paths, gradients, fills, strokes, text, dashed lines, ellipses, circles) produce identical output in both SKGLView (WebGL) and SKCanvasView (software). The reporter's version (2.88.x preview from 2022) could not be tested due to .NET 6 JSInterop API dependency, but the issue appears resolved in SkiaSharp 3.x — likely due to improved WebGL handling in SkiaSharp and improved WebGL2 support in iOS Safari since 2022.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WASM project with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.9 (closest to reporter's preview.256)
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 2.88.9

reproductionSteps.description: Build and run 2.88.9 project — runtime TypeLoadException because 2.88.9 Blazor views depend on IJSUnmarshalledObjectReference removed in .NET 7+. This is a version compatibility issue, not the reported bug.
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded but runtime error: System.TypeLoadException: Could not resolve type with token 01000039 from typeref (expected class Microsoft.JSInterop.IJSUnmarshalledObjectReference). Cannot test reporter's version on .NET 10.

reproductionSteps.description: Create fresh Blazor WASM project with latest SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.2 and WasmBuildNative enabled. Repro page has both SKGLView and SKCanvasView drawing identical complex shapes (columns with gradients, paths, ellipses, circles, dashed lines, text labels).
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p $TMPDIR/repro/2049-latest && cd $TMPDIR/repro/2049-latest && dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.2

filesCreated.description: Blazor page with SKGLView and SKCanvasView side-by-side, both calling the same DrawContent method that draws chemical-engineering-style columns (gradient-filled rects, internal tray lines, feed inlet paths, condenser ellipse, reboiler circle, connecting cubic bezier pipe with dashed effect, valve symbol path, and text labels).

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor WASM project with native compilation
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s) 0 Error(s). Time Elapsed 00:00:07.25

reproductionSteps.description: Serve app and verify in desktop Chromium via Playwright. Both SKGLView and SKCanvasView render all elements identically. Browser console shows GL_RENDER and CANVAS_RENDER success messages. Two benign WebGL warnings about WEBGL_debug_renderer_info (Chrome privacy feature, not a bug).
reproductionSteps.output: Console: GL_RENDER: 1196x796, CANVAS_RENDER: 1196x796. WebGL: INVALID_ENUM: getParameter: invalid parameter name, WEBGL_debug_renderer_info not enabled (benign). Screenshot confirms both views render identical chemical column diagrams.

reproductionSteps.description: Test on iOS Safari via iPhone 16 Pro Simulator. Booted simulator, opened Safari to localhost:5112. SKGLView renders all elements correctly — columns with gradients, internal trays, condenser, reboiler, connecting pipe, valve symbol, and text labels all visible. SKCanvasView heading and top of its canvas also visible (identical green condenser ellipse).
reproductionSteps.command: xcrun simctl boot 38BA64E9-7160-44C7-94CE-36B9F5C2F424 && xcrun simctl openurl 38BA64E9-7160-44C7-94CE-36B9F5C2F424 http://localhost:5112 && sleep 30 && xcrun simctl io 38BA64E9-7160-44C7-94CE-36B9F5C2F424 screenshot ios-safari.png
reproductionSteps.output: iOS Safari Simulator screenshot confirms SKGLView renders all drawing elements correctly. No missing elements observed — all shapes, gradients, paths, text labels visible.

versionResults.notes: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 2.88.9 targets net6.0 and uses IJSUnmarshalledObjectReference, removed in .NET 7+. TypeLoadException at runtime on .NET 10. Cannot install wasm-tools-net6 without elevated privileges.
Both SKGLView and SKCanvasView render all elements correctly on desktop Chromium and iOS Safari Simulator (iPhone 16 Pro). WebGL INVALID_ENUM warnings about WEBGL_debug_renderer_info are benign.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this. We attempted to reproduce the SKGLView rendering issue on iOS Safari with the latest SkiaSharp 3.119.2.

Using a Blazor WASM app with both `SKGLView` (WebGL) and `SKCanvasView` (software) drawing the same complex shapes (columns with gradients, paths, ellipses, dashed lines, text), both views render identically on:
- Desktop Chromium (macOS)
- iOS Safari Simulator (iPhone 16 Pro)

Since this was reported against an early 2022 preview version, the issue was likely resolved through improvements in both SkiaSharp's Blazor WebGL integration and iOS Safari's WebGL2 support.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Platform | Result |
|---------|----------|--------|
| 2.88.9 | Blazor WASM | ⚠️ Incompatible with .NET 10 (net6.0-only JSInterop APIs) |
| 3.119.2 | Desktop Chromium | ✅ Both views render correctly |
| 3.119.2 | iOS Safari Simulator | ✅ Both views render correctly |

Could you try upgrading to SkiaSharp 3.119.2? If the issue persists with your specific drawing code, could you share a minimal reproduction project?
proposedResponse.summary: SKGLView rendering issue on iOS Safari not reproduced with SkiaSharp 3.119.2

actionability.reason: The SKGLView rendering issue on iOS Safari does not reproduce with SkiaSharp 3.119.2. Both SKGLView (WebGL) and SKCanvasView (software) render identically on desktop Chromium and iOS Safari Simulator. The original report was against a 2022 preview version; improvements in both SkiaSharp's WebGL handling and iOS Safari's WebGL2 support likely resolved this.

workarounds: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.119.2 which resolves the SKGLView rendering on iOS Safari
Use SKCanvasView instead of SKGLView as a fallback (software rendering, no WebGL dependency)

actions.description: Post reproduction findings showing the issue is resolved on latest SkiaSharp
actions.comment: Tested SKGLView vs SKCanvasView rendering on SkiaSharp 3.119.2 with both desktop Chromium and iOS Safari Simulator — both views render identically with no missing elements.

missingInfo: Confirmation from reporter whether the issue persists with SkiaSharp 3.x
Minimal reproduction project if issue still occurs on current versions
